change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value:
Participatory planning, including revision and rolling process of community plans presented to LAs and other stakeholders on an annual basis in all slums in the 3 towns where we have worked before, and in selected slums in the other 3 towns.
	
		Establishment or strengthening of 2 town-wide slum dweller organisations
	
		Capacity building training, and assessment of capacity and representativeness of the local organisations through annual review processes and tools for self-assessment
	
		Particular focus on needs specific to the communities we are working with, and to marginalised sections within those communities.
	
		Building the capacity of LA officials and elected representatives, and establishing systems so that expertise and approaches are passed on effectively to new post-holders
	
		city-level forums for co-ordination between stakeholders, giving priority to the needs of slum dwellers, and allocating resources to support their priorities
	
		City-level working groups established or strengthened to deal with specific issues
	
		Leverage of resources to support community infrastructure priorities from LAs, national programmes and other stakeholders, with the proportion contributed by project resources declining over the course of the project
	
		Delivery and management of community infrastructure services (water supply, improved drainage, cluster-latrines, internal roads and paths etc)
	
		Exchange visits and other tools which help communities to effectively choose and adapt the types of technologies most appropriate to their situations
	
		Promoting the uptake of household-level infrastructure (e.g. household latrines, biogas, home composting, water vending, and incremental housing improvements)
	
		Income generation through service delivery with small enterprises in, latrine construction, pit emptying, and waste collection and recycling
	
		Peer-learning workshops and building relationships
	
		Sharing knowledge and good practices with national and regional municipal associations, networks of NGOs, and slum dweller networks
	
		Providing materials (case studies and training modules) for national and regional training institutes
	
		Influencing nationwide programmes to adopt elements of the project’s approach for wider scaling up
	
		Project regional workshops and visits for synthesising lessons from the 3 countries (Nepal, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka)
	
		Dissemination of lessons regionally and internationally through conferences and publications

snapshot.implementing_agency: Lumanti Support Group for Shelter, Environment and Public Health Organization

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
